sections_cited: Sections 194-A and 194-B of the Customs Act

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH KARACHI C.P. No.D-7772 of 2022 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGES Priority 1. For hearing of Misc. No.32940 of 2022 2. For hearing of main case 21.05.2025 Mr. Muhammad Rizwan, Advocate holds brief for Mr. Amjad Hayat, Advocate for Petitioner Mr. Muhammad Khalil Dogar, Advocate for Respondent No.2 ************ This is a petition, seeking implementation of an order passed by the Customs Appellate Tribunal, at Karachi. It appears that vide order dated 09.8.2024 passed by the Hon’ble Supreme Court1, whereby it has been held the Customs Appellate Tribunal has the power to execute orders passed by it while exercising jurisdiction under Sections 194-A and 194-B of the Customs Act, 1969, and consequently, since an adequate remedy is provided by law, the writ jurisdiction of the High Court cannot be invoked for executing orders passed by the Tribunal. In view of such position, this Petition is dismissed as not maintainable with pending application, whereas the Petitioner is at liberty to seek appropriate remedy as observed in the above order of the Hon’ble Supreme Court. ACTING CHIEF JUSTICE JUDGE Qurban 1 In Civil Petition No.3391/2024 [Khalid alias Muhammad Khalid and others v. Collector of Customs (Adjudication), Customs House, Lahore, etc.],
